violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. PIC has application completed and stated will bring to RODOH CFP office today. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. PIC has completed food safety course and has RIDOH application. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2024
Restricted pesticide (Boric Acid) was observed within establishment. PIC informed to cease use and disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings, roach carcasses & droppings, and live roaches. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the electrical boxes and outlets have an accumulation of insect fecal matter and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety Licensed with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. FMC expired 2021. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

Raw animal foods (raw shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC moved eggs during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the dish machine sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC contacted service during inspection; technician arrived during inspection. All dishes and utensils to be sanitized in 3-bay sink until dish machine is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

Chicken and tuna salad were observed held at 46-65 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep units. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, portioned chicken was recently cooked; sliced chicken recently cut; tuna salad was prepared from room temperature canned tuna. Foods were taken to freezer to rapidly chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, chicken was placed warm in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep, covered with plastic wrap. Inspector reviewed cooling procedures with PIC; PIC uncovered containers to facilitate further cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings within facility. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC stated that whole building, including all tenants, receives treatment once per month. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024
The dish machine was observed not dispensing sanitizer solution at the time of inspection. PIC contacted service during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024
Two outside dumpsters were not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

There were several pallets in the refuse area and trash debris behind dumpsters at the time of inspection. Refuse areas must be maintained clean to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024
